name: Nike Baseball Camp at Westmont College - All Skills Camp (July 19–22, 2026)
description: Come join the fun at the Westmont College Nike Baseball Camp! Gorgeous weather, superb instruction, and great competition makes this a wonderful baseball camp experience for athletes of all abilities. Directing the camp is Tom Myers who serves as the Professional Area Scout for the Los Angeles Dodgers, covering from San Luis Obispo to Long Beach CA. Our goal is for every camper to take their game to the next level, create lasting friendships, and have a good time.

name: Nike Baseball Camp Montreat College -  (July 13–17, 2026)
description: Nike Baseball Camp is heading back to Montreat College for summer 2026, and we can’t wait! Camp will be led by Jason Beck, Montreat’s Head Coach and a seasoned Nike Camp Director who knows how to bring out the best in young players. Each day is all about learning, growing, and having fun—with drills that cover hitting, fielding, base running, and team play. Plus, kids get to play on Newell Field, the home of Cavaliers Baseball. It’s a great way for your child to build skills, confidence, and make some great summer memories!

name: Nike Baseball Camp at Boise State University - Day Camp (June 15–18, 2026)
description: Nike Baseball Camps is excited to return to Boise State University in 2026! Register today and enjoy elite-level training while making memories to last a lifetime. The camp offers a unique opportunity to train under the direction of passionate coaches and current and former collegiate athletes who serve as counselors. The camp is open to athletes ages 10-17. Overnight Camp Our overnight camp at Boise State offers an immersive, on-campus experience where campers reside in the beautiful BSU dormitories (just steps from the Boise River) in adjacent rooms to the camp staff while training during the daytime hours. The tuition includes housing, meals, fun evening activities, as well as college recruiting seminars on Tuesday evening (parents are encouraged to attend the seminars!). Day Camp (9:00am – 4:00pm) Nike Baseball Camps also offers a summer day camp program for local athletes. This program runs 9:00am-4:00pm (schedule varies by day) and includes daytime training only. Campers can bring their own lunch or purchase it on campus. Nike Sports Camp Experience At Boise State University, Nike Sports Camps offers a unique experience with many sports (Baseball, Cross Country, Golf, Soccer, Softball, Swim, Tennis, and Track & Field) operating simultaneously. During the day, the athletes receive sport-specific coaching within their sport focus. In the evening, overnight campers across all sports come together for a special camp-wide experience which includes fun field games, educational seminars, and more. Baseball Complex/Transportation Information Train like the pros in 2026! The Nike Baseball Camp at BSU will now be conducting all baseball training at Memorial Stadium in Garden City—the official home of the Boise Hawks. Campers and staff will be transported by bus between Boise State and the stadium each day. The Nike Baseball Camp at Boise State University is open to any and all entrants and limited only by number, age and gender. Athletic facilities and coaches are subject to change. The camp is not owned, sponsored, controlled or endorsed by Boise State University.

name: Nike Baseball Camp at Elmira College -  (July 6–9, 2026)
description: The Nike Baseball Camp at Elmira College is thrilled to be back for the 2026 camp season! name: Nike Baseball Camp With Legacy 3 42 Outlaws at Rockford University -  (June 8–11, 2026)
description: This is a development-first baseball camp built around individual prospect development. Day one will focus on onboarding and baseline evaluation using the Athletic Information Assessment (AIA). Each camper will receive an AIA, which serves as their Player Development Profile (PDP), a framework widely used by MLB and USA Baseball to evaluate and guide long-term prospect development. That information will be used to individualize instruction throughout the camp. On-field training will emphasize hitting, fielding, base running, game awareness, and movement efficiency.

name: Nike Baseball Camp at Route 66 Stadium, directed by Springfield Cardinals -  (July 28–30, 2026)
description: This summer, get ready for an unforgettable experience with the Springfield Cardinals, the Double-A Affiliate of the St. Louis Cardinals, at the Nike Baseball Camp at Route 66 Stadium in Springfield, MO. name: Nike Baseball Camp at Union University -  (July 13–16, 2026)
description: Join us this summer for the Nike Baseball Camp at Union University in Jackson, Tennessee! This new camp offers a fun and engaging opportunity for young athletes to develop their baseball skills in a positive and instruction-focused environment. The camp will run July 13–16 from 9:00am–3:00pm and is open to boys ages 7–14. Designed for beginner and intermediate players, this camp welcomes athletes of all abilities who are eager to learn, improve, and enjoy the game. Throughout the week, campers will train with experienced coaches and players who are passionate about teaching the fundamentals of baseball. Daily instruction will include skill development in hitting, pitching, catching, infield and outfield play, baserunning, and defensive fundamentals. Campers will work through drills and position-specific instruction that focuses on proper mechanics, improving baseball IQ, and building confidence on the field. In addition to skill development, campers will take part in games, competitions, and fun challenge-based activities designed to reinforce teamwork, toughness, and a competitive spirit. Coaches will emphasize situational awareness, smart decision-making, and competing with the right attitude while creating an energetic and supportive atmosphere. The Nike Baseball Camp at Union University is built to help players grow both as athletes and teammates. Whether your child is just beginning their baseball journey or looking to sharpen their skills, this camp provides a fun and encouraging environment where players can improve, build confidence, and develop a passion for the game

name: Nike Baseball Camp in Toms River - All Skills Camp (July 27–30, 2026)
description: Get ready for an awesome summer at Nike Baseball Camp Toms River 2026! Join Director Chris Langan and his top-notch coaching staff for a week packed with fun, learning, and serious skill-building. You’ll level up your game with drills focused on hitting, fielding, team play, and real game situations—all while learning how to stay sharp mentally on the field. Whether you’re looking to take your game to the next level or just want to have a great time playing baseball, this camp in Toms River, NJ is the place to be! Coach Chris Langan brings a ton of experience and success to the field—he’s a four-time Central Atlantic Collegiate Conference Coach of the Year and has led Felician University to multiple championships, playoff runs, and record-breaking seasons. Under his leadership, the Golden Falcons have become one of the top programs in the conference, known for their consistency, strong fundamentals, and competitive spirit.

name: Nike Baseball Camp at Saint Joseph’s University -  (June 29–July 2, 2026)
description: Join us this summer at John W. This camp provides young athletes with high-level instruction, competitive drills, and a fun training environment designed to elevate their game. Coach Hamburg and his staff will offer two tailored divisions to ensure players train at the right pace and skill level: Beginner Division: Boys ages 7–11 who are newer to the game and looking to build strong fundamentals.Intermediate Division: Boys ages 12–14 with prior playing experience who want to sharpen their skills and improve their baseball IQ.Please note: This camp is not intended for high school players. name: Nike Baseball Camp in The Hamptons -  (Aug. 24–28, 2026)
description: Join Nike Baseball Camps in Mashashimuet Park (Sag Harbor), hosted by New York Empire Baseball. This intensive 5-day Camp will group players by both age and skill, and players will be trained by some of the most well-trained and certified coaches in youth baseball - using the most advanced technology and engaging and in-depth instruction available. The Camp will be led by New York Empire Baseball's advanced instructional staff - one of the most highly trained and certified in the baseball industry. The coaching staff will be led by Director of Research & Training, Coach Jordan Baltimore. Coach Baltimore was the world’s first coach to receive Driveline Certification, is on the Blast Motion Advisory Board, and is also certified by OnBaseU, K-Motion, and Rapsodo Sports. He's also the PItching Consultant to the NYU Langone Sports Performance Center, and was named to the New York City Mayor's Baseball Advisory Commission at the Office of Sports, Wellness, and Recreation. He was previously the New York Area Director for the National Pitching Association. Instructors will utilize New York Empire Baseball's proprietary Player Development Platform along with Blast Motion Bat Sensors and high-speed video analysis, among other technologies, to measure, assess, and define unique development programs for every player. Players will begin their week-long experience with a complete Assessment and Baseline Hitting & Pitching analysis before receiving their personalized Camp training program. All players will then engage in 5 days of small group sessions in Hitting, Throwing, Infielding, Outfielding, Baserunning, and Game Situations. Each day will focus on healthy athletic development and skill acquisition in a fun & engaging environment that will also teach the mental/emotional approach needed to eliminate performance anxiety and fear of failure. Players will develop skills that scale, and receive an individualized post-Camp training routine to continue their own development.

name: Nike Baseball Camp at University of La Verne -  (June 22–25, 2026)
description: Get ready for the Nike Baseball Camps at the University of La Verne in Southern California! All baseball players that attend will learn valuable skills that they can transfer into their game to help them become better baseball players. Instruction will cover all aspects of baseball including fielding, hitting, baserunning, and team play. Summer is the perfect time to continue perfecting your love of the game and Director Scott Winterburn is confident he can help your camper reach their goals. Scott Winterburn, who took over the University of La Verne baseball program in 2001 after coaching at Azusa Pacific University, has built a powerhouse program over his tenure. Under his leadership, the Leopards have captured eight SCIAC titles (2004, 2005, 2008, 2012, 2018, 2023, 2024, and 2025), earned multiple Top 20 national rankings, made several NCAA tournament appearances, two super regional appearances (2023 and 2024), and two SCIAC Postseason Tournament Championships (2015 and 2025). Winterburn has also developed numerous standout players, coaching six SCIAC Players or Pitchers of the Year—all of whom earned All-America honors—and producing 43 All-West Region selections and 9 total All-Americans.

name: Nike Baseball Camp at On-Deck in Prince George’s County -  (July 6–10, 2026)
description: The Nike Baseball Camp at On-Deck in Prince George’s County offers a high-level training experience in an intimate, athlete-focused environment. Directed by Andre Hockaday and the professional coaching staff at On-Deck, this camp is designed for players who want focused instruction, personalized feedback, and serious skill development—without losing the fun of the game. This summer, the camp will run four weekly sessions throughout the month of July, with camp held Monday through Friday from 9:00am–3:00pm. Each week features four days of skill development inside the state-of-the-art On-Deck indoor facility, followed by a final day of live scrimmages and gameplay on the turf baseball field at Prince George’s Community College. The On-Deck facility is a 7,000-square-foot, air-conditioned training space equipped with a large indoor turf field, five batting cages, and modern baseball training technology. With just 50 campers per week, athletes receive extensive one-on-one instruction and small-group coaching from experienced professional baseball coaches and trainers. Campers can expect a well-rounded experience that blends advanced skill development, competitive gameplay, and a positive, high-energy atmosphere—all built to help players grow their confidence, sharpen their skills, and enjoy every day at camp.
